reasoning: While programmatic text recall is high (shingling recall 0.948), there is a significant discrepancy regarding visual content. The LLM fidelity report identifies a major failure in image preservation, specifically citing the loss of critical 'before and after' screenshots and accessibility diagrams in a document specifically about a website redesign. Because these images are core to the document's utility and the signals disagree on the routing (0.6 fidelity vs. 0.97 pipeline confidence), a human must evaluate the visual accessibility.

concerns.analysis: The vast majority of meaningful images from the source document are missing from the HTML output. This includes critical evidence of the website redesign such as 'before and after' screenshots (pages 12, 15, 18, 21, 23, 26), accessibility tool screenshots (pages 13, 14, 16, 17), and conceptual diagrams (pages 8, 9, 24). Since the entire document is about a visual redesign and accessibility improvements, these images are highly meaningful and their absence significantly degrades the document's ut
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Side-by-side screenshots of old and new website (page 12); 'Evolution of the Mobile Phone' image (page 8); accessibility button contrast examples (page 14); '44 rule' touch target diagrams (page 24).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output shows only the title headings for these sections followed by text lists or empty space, with no corresponding elements for the aforementioned screenshots.
concerns.location: Sections: 'New Web - Mobile First and Mobile Device Use', 'Changes to SOS Website Before and After', 'Accessibility - Button Contrast', 'Accessibility Challenges Old Website', 'Improved Presentation on New Site', '44 rule'

concerns.analysis: Several section headings are visually duplicated in the main flow of the HTML document. Each affected heading appears twice consecutively—once as a primary header and once as a sub-header—without any content between them. This occurs for sections like 'Follow Federal Template' and 'Changes to SOS Website Before and After'.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Follow Federal Template (page 5); Comply with ADA Title II Accessibility Requirements (page 7); New Web - Mobile First and Mobile Device Use (page 8); Changes to SOS Website Before and After (page 11).
concerns.output_quote: The headings 'Follow Federal Template', 'Comply with ADA Title II Accessibility Requirements', 'New Web - Mobile First and Mobile Device Use', and 'Changes to SOS Website Before and After' each appear twice in immediate succession.
concerns.location: Main body content after 'Modernize KB Technology' and 'Comply with ADA Title II Accessibility Requirements' sections.

action_items: Verify if the missing screenshots are replaced with adequate alt-text or if there is a total loss of visual information.
Determine if the duplicated headings interfere with screen reader navigation.
Assess if the HTML is useful without the visual 'before and after' comparisons that form the core of the presentation.
